primary2026.notes: Bob Brooks (D), president of the PA Fire Fighters Association, won the Democratic primary. Ryan Mackenzie (R) is the Republican nominee. Both advance to the Nov. 3, 2026 general election.

Pennsylvania statewide primary. Polls open 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM.

RULES.currentRule: Online, mail, or in-person registration through PA Voter Services or a county election office. Must be a U.S. citizen, PA resident in the district at least 30 days, and 18+ by election day.
RULES.whyItMatters: Determines who can cast a ballot in PA-07.
RULES.detail: Registration applications must be received at least 15 days before the election. Primary deadline May 4, 2026; general deadline Oct. 19, 2026.

RULES.currentRule: Any registered Pennsylvania voter may apply for a no-excuse mail-in ballot. No reason required.
RULES.whyItMatters: Mail-in ballots are a primary voting channel for many PA-07 voters.

RULES.currentRule: Absentee ballots are limited to voters who meet a qualifying reason (illness, disability, absence from the municipality, etc.). Voters who don't need a reason should use a mail-in ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: Distinguishes the two PA mail-voting tracks and which application a voter should use.

RULES.currentRule: Mail-in and absentee ballot applications must be received by the county election office by 5:00 PM seven days before election day.
RULES.whyItMatters: Late applications cannot be processed; voters must use in-person voting if missed.
RULES.detail: Primary: May 12, 2026 at 5:00 PM. General: Oct. 27, 2026 at 5:00 PM.

RULES.currentRule: Voted mail-in and absentee ballots must be received by the county election office by 8:00 PM on election day. Postmarks are not sufficient.
RULES.whyItMatters: Late receipt is the most common rejection reason for mail-voted ballots.

RULES.currentRule: Polls are open 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM on election day across Pennsylvania.
RULES.whyItMatters: Defines the window for in-person voting in PA-07.

RULES.currentRule: First-time voters at a polling place, and voters voting at a new polling place for the first time, are required to show acceptable ID. Returning voters at their established polling place generally do not need to show ID.
RULES.whyItMatters: Affects in-person eligibility, especially for newly registered or recently moved voters.
RULES.detail: Sample/unverified summary. Confirm exact acceptable-ID list and first-time-voter rule against PA Title 25 and PA Department of State guidance before public release.

RULES.currentRule: Pennsylvania law requires voters to handwrite a date on the mail-ballot outer envelope. Whether undated or misdated envelopes are counted has been the subject of ongoing litigation.
RULES.whyItMatters: Affects whether a returned mail ballot is counted in close PA-07 contests.
RULES.detail: Watch item — see Conflicts panel. Spotlight PA explainer and PA Department of State guidance should be reconciled before public reliance.

RULES.currentRule: Voters can track their mail-in or absentee ballot status through PA Voter Services from application through receipt at the county election office.
RULES.whyItMatters: Lets voters confirm their ballot was received in time and resolve issues before deadlines.

RULES.currentRule: Pennsylvania elections are administered at the county level. Mail-in and absentee applications, ballots, and in-person logistics flow through each county's election office.
RULES.whyItMatters: PA-07 voters interact with Carbon, Lehigh, Northampton, or Monroe county election offices depending on residence.

RULES.currentRule: PA-07 includes all of Carbon, Lehigh, and Northampton counties and parts of Monroe County under the maps used for the 2022–2030 cycle.
RULES.whyItMatters: Determines who is a constituent of PA-07.
RULES.detail: District composition cited from Ballotpedia (district context / secondary). Cross-check against an official PA redistricting / map source before public reliance.

UPDATES.title: Primary mail-in / absentee request deadline confirmed
UPDATES.fieldChanged: Primary mail-in / absentee ballot request deadline

UPDATES.title: PA ballot receipt rule restated: postmarks not sufficient
UPDATES.newValue: Ballots must be received by the county election office by 8:00 PM on election day; postmarks do not count.

UPDATES.title: Mail-ballot envelope-dating rule flagged as watch item
UPDATES.oldValue: Treated as a routine procedural step
UPDATES.newValue: Marked as a watch item; pending PA court guidance on undated/misdated envelopes

UPDATES.title: Carbon County direct elections URL still missing
UPDATES.newValue: Routed to PA county-contact directory as a placeholder until verified

UPDATES.title: PA-07 news list refreshed with VoteBeat Pennsylvania prioritized
UPDATES.oldValue: 4 news items, with older sample-style entries
UPDATES.newValue: 10 newest-to-oldest voting-procedure news items; current VoteBeat Pennsylvania primary story placed first

UPDATES.title: PA-07 primary results added — Brooks (D) and Mackenzie (R) confirmed as general election nominees
UPDATES.newValue: Bob Brooks (D) won Democratic primary May 19; Ryan Mackenzie (R) is Republican nominee. Both advance to Nov. 3, 2026 general election.

UPDATES.title: General-election deadlines re-verified against PA Department of State; no changes detected
UPDATES.newValue: As of the June 17, 2026 refresh, PA Department of State guidance still lists October 19, 2026 as the voter registration deadline, October 27, 2026 as the mail-in/absentee application deadline, and November 3, 2026 as Election Day with mail ballots due by 8:00 PM. Postmarks do not count. Envelope-dating signature requirement still in effect per PA DoS mail-ballot guidance.

UPDATES.title: SCOTUS denied RNC emergency request on PA provisional-ballot backstop (June 6, 2026)
UPDATES.oldValue: Provisional-ballot backstop under PA Supreme Court ruling in Center for Coalfield Justice v. Washington County Board of Elections still operative; awaiting any federal challenge.
UPDATES.newValue: On June 6, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court denied the RNC's emergency request to block PA from counting provisional ballots cast by voters whose mail-in ballots were invalid due to envelope errors. Denial was procedural — the case (limited to two voters in one county) was not a proper vehicle for statewide relief. Justices Alito, Thomas, and Gorsuch wrote separately leaving the underlying constitutional question open. The Coalfield Justice provisional-ballot backstop remains operative for November 3, 2026.

CONFLICTS.factInQuestion: Whether undated or misdated mail-ballot outer envelopes are counted
CONFLICTS.sourceAClaim: Spotlight PA explainer summarizes ongoing court cases that have changed whether undated or misdated outer envelopes are counted.
CONFLICTS.sourceBClaim: PA Department of State guidance instructs voters to date the outer envelope; does not provide a current single-rule resolution for litigation outcomes.
CONFLICTS.whyItMatters: Determines whether a returned mail ballot in PA-07 is counted in close contests; affects voter guidance language.
CONFLICTS.suggestedReviewAction: Defer to PA Department of State and PA courts for the controlling rule; treat news explainer as context only.

CONFLICTS.sourceAClaim: No direct Carbon County elections page verified for Milestone 1; routed to PA county-contact directory.
CONFLICTS.sourceBClaim: PA Department of State directory lists county-level contact information for Carbon County, but is not a direct elections page.
CONFLICTS.whyItMatters: Risk of sending Carbon County voters to a generic directory instead of a direct county elections page. Should be replaced with a verified URL in Milestone 2.
CONFLICTS.suggestedReviewAction: Verify and add a direct Carbon County elections URL; mark the placeholder source resolved once added.

NEWS.headline: SCOTUS responses due June 1 in PA mail-ballot dating case — ruling could affect November
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Responses in Commonwealth v. Eakin (No. 25-967) were due at SCOTUS on June 1, 2026. If the Court takes the case and reverses the 3rd Circuit, counties may again be allowed to reject undated mail ballots — directly affecting PA-07 general election mail voters.

NEWS.headline: Bob Brooks wins Democratic nomination in hotly contested PA-07 primary
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Bob Brooks (D), president of the PA Fire Fighters Association and Gov. Shapiro's preferred candidate, won the May 19 Democratic primary for PA-07 with 41.4% of the vote. Ryan Mackenzie (R) advances as the Republican nominee. Brooks vs. Mackenzie is the November 3 general election matchup.

NEWS.headline: PA's 2026 primary election goes well despite a few isolated incidents
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Statewide primary ran without major machine malfunctions or printing errors. Isolated incidents in York and Chester counties affected a few hundred voters but did not disrupt statewide results. Turnout was low — under 20% of registered voters.

NEWS.headline: Uncertainty for PA counties after court rules on releasing vote data
NEWS.relevanceSummary: PA Supreme Court unanimously ruled CVRs are public records (April 2026, Heather Honey v. Lycoming County). But how many PA counties can actually release them depends on tabulation technology — only 8 counties use Clear Ballot systems with ballot randomization. Lehigh and Northampton (PA-07 counties) may face different timelines.

NEWS.headline: Here's how Pennsylvania's primary has been going so far
NEWS.relevanceSummary: VoteBeat's primary update covers Pennsylvania mail-ballot issues, election process changes, and technology/testing items relevant to PA-07 voter guidance.

NEWS.headline: May 15 update on Lehigh County mail balloting ahead of the 5/19 primary
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Official county update says a clerical error affected some initial mail ballots and explains the affected voter categories and county remedies.

NEWS.headline: Pennsylvania 2026 primary voter guide explains deadlines and voting rules
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Voter guide covers primary deadlines, registration status checks, mail-ballot tracking, and polling-place guidance useful to PA-07 voters.

NEWS.headline: Last-minute guide to voting in the 2026 Pennsylvania primary election
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Explains practical voting rules for the May 19 primary, including mail-ballot deadlines, ID for first-time voters, and how voters can check county resources.

NEWS.headline: PA Department of State reminds voters of the one-week mail ballot application deadline
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Official state reminder highlights the May 12 mail-ballot application deadline, the May 19 8:00 PM receipt deadline, and the rule that postmarks do not count.

NEWS.headline: Pennsylvania secretary of the commonwealth answers election questions at House budget hearing
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Coverage of state election administration issues, including the SURE system, special elections, and AI-related election concerns.

NEWS.headline: Pennsylvania to test out internet-connected e-pollbooks this spring
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Explains the state pilot for internet-connected electronic pollbooks, a voting-technology change that could affect election-day administration in PA-07.

NEWS.headline: Fight over Pennsylvania's mail-ballot date requirement approaches the endgame
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Explains the long-running legal fight over whether the handwritten date on mail-ballot envelopes can be enforced — still a watch item ahead of November 3.

NEWS.headline: Enforcing date requirement for Pennsylvania mail ballots is ruled unconstitutional
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Reports the 3rd Circuit ruling in the mail-ballot envelope-dating litigation, which remains a watch item (SCOTUS petition Commonwealth v. Eakin, responses due June 1, 2026).

NEWS.headline: PA primary results: Brooks (D) wins 4-way primary to face Mackenzie (R) in PA-07 toss-up
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Statewide primary recap confirming Bob Brooks won the four-way Democratic primary against Crosswell, McClure, and Obando-Derstine; will face incumbent Ryan Mackenzie (R) on Nov. 3. Sabato's Crystal Ball rates the race a toss-up.

NEWS.headline: SCOTUS denies RNC emergency request to block PA provisional-ballot backstop
NEWS.relevanceSummary: On June 6, 2026, SCOTUS denied the RNC's emergency request to stop PA from counting provisional ballots from voters whose mail-in ballots had envelope errors. Denial was procedural (case was limited to two voters in one county). Alito/Thomas/Gorsuch wrote separately leaving the constitutional question open.

LAST_REFRESH.summary: June 17, 2026 bi-weekly refresh for PA-07. Re-verified PA Department of State upcoming-elections and mail-in/absentee guidance directly: general-election deadlines unchanged (Oct. 19 registration, Oct. 27 mail-application, Nov. 3 Election Day and 8:00 PM mail-ballot receipt; postmarks do not count; envelope-dating signature requirement still in effect). Brooks (D) vs. Mackenzie (R) remains the Nov. 3 matchup. New: documented June 6, 2026 SCOTUS denial of RNC emergency request on PA provisional-ballot backstop (Coalfield Justice rule survives). Commonwealth v. Eakin (envelope-dating) still pending at SCOTUS. Watson v. RNC (Mississippi/Alaska late ballots) decision expected end of June; PA receipt-by-8pm rule unaffected directly. All lastVerified dates bumped to 2026-06-17. No confidence flips.

WEEKLY_REPORT.summary: Bi-weekly period June 3–17, 2026: General-election deadlines re-verified directly against PA DoS — unchanged. Brooks (D) vs. Mackenzie (R) confirmed for Nov. 3. New litigation watch: SCOTUS denied RNC emergency request (June 6) on PA provisional-ballot backstop — Coalfield Justice rule survives. Commonwealth v. Eakin (envelope-dating) still pending. No procedure changes detected; no confidence flips.
